title: podman machine init fails on M1 with Ventura update
body: **Is this a or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** I can't boot podman after I updated the OS to Ventura on M1. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Run brew install podman && podman machine init **Describe the results you received:** ``` $ podman machine init Error: podman-machine-default: VM already exists ``` **Subsequent call results in:** ``` $ podman machine start Starting machine "podman-machine-default" Error: dial unix /var/folders/_p/h1g79x650q7b325c6hqrxvhh0000gn/T/podman/qmp_podman-machine-default.sock: connect: no such file or directory ``` **Describe the results you expected:** Having running usable podman VM on Apple Silicon. **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** N/A **Output of `podman version`:** ``` $ podman version Error: failed to connect: dial tcp [::1]:51512: connect: connection refused ``` **Output of `podman info`:** ``` $ podman info Error: failed to connect: dial tcp [::1]:51512: connect: connection refused ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):** ``` Tool for managing OCI containers and pods https://podman.io/ /opt/homebrew/Cellar/podman/4.3.0 (185 files, 47.6MB) * Poured from bottle on 2022-11-07 at 08:45:20 From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/podman.rb License: Apache-2.0 and GPL-3.0-or-later ==> Dependencies Build: go-md2man ✘, go@1.18 ✘ Required: qemu ✔ ==> Options --HEAD Install HEAD version ==> Caveats zsh completions have been installed to: /opt/homebrew/share/zsh/site-functions To restart podman after an upgrade: brew services restart podman Or, if you don't want/need a background service you can just run: /opt/homebrew/opt/podman/bin/podman system service --time=0 ==> Analytics install: 26,761 (30 days), 76,262 (90 days), 224,129 (365 days) install-on-request: 25,612 (30 days), 73,637 (90 days), 221,032 (365 days) build-error: 19 (30 days) ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)** Yes **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** N/A

comments.body: Looks like you have an existing machine from a previous install. If you are not particularly attached to your current machine, try podman machine rm and re-creating your podman machine.

Now, init works, but start not:
```
$ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: dial unix /var/folders/5n/z9q6q3hn5fndnb1hw27yhw1w0000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default_ready.sock: connect: no such file or directory
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
It looks like you r connection data is wrong or the socket is not listening
I ended up giving up on the tool :(
Sorry to hear it, would love to have you try us a again at some point, along with pdoman-desktop.
